steps.observed: Lines 103-110: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS facts_fts USING fts5(fact, category, compressed_details, content='facts', content_rowid='id', tokenize='porter unicode61')

steps.expected: All three triggers include compressed_details in their INSERT statements
steps.observed: facts_ai (line 131): INSERT INTO facts_fts(rowid, fact, category, compressed_details) VALUES (new.id, new.fact, new.category, new.compressed_details); facts_au (lines 136-139): Both DELETE and INSERT include compressed_details for old and new values; facts_ad (lines 143-144): DELETE includes compressed_details for old values

assertions.title: Migration adds compressed_details to existing DB

steps.expected: Function adds compressed_details column if missing and handles FTS5 rebuild (drop+recreate facts_fts)
steps.observed: migrate_schema() in memory_db.py: (1) Migration 2 at line ~30 checks columns via PRAGMA table_info and runs ALTER TABLE facts ADD COLUMN compressed_details TEXT if missing. (2) Migration 3 checks if facts_fts includes compressed_details by inspecting sqlite_master sql; if not, sets needs_fts_rebuild=True. (3) When needs_fts_rebuild: drops triggers (facts_ai, facts_au, facts_ad), drops facts_fts table, recreates facts_fts with compressed_details, recreates all three triggers with compressed_details, and runs INSERT INTO facts_fts(facts_fts) VALUES('rebuild').

keyFeatures: ALTER TABLE facts ADD COLUMN compressed_details TEXT
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE facts_fts with compressed_details
FTS rebuild via INSERT INTO facts_fts(facts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')

steps.action: Read the top of schema.sql for migration instructions comment block
steps.expected: SQL comment block with migration instructions for upgrading existing databases
Command succeeds with exit code 0 and no error output
steps.observed: Command completed with EXIT_CODE=0 and no error output

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked, 0 skipped. All schema assertions for the distill-and-schema milestone are verified.
